div1.entryFree: Sanguine, having the colour of blood, red: this s. coward, H4A II, 4, 268 (red-faced with much drinking). the s. colour of the leaves (of the rose) H6A IV, 1, 92. ye s. shallow-hearted boys, ye white-limed walls, ye ale-house painted signs, coal-black is better than another hue, Tit. IV, 2, 97. had upon his neck a mole, a s. star, Cymb. V, 5, 364.
